title: Add 30-minute lunch deduction to working-hour calculation

requirements.text: Update the working-hour calculation logic in `frontend/src/utils/hoursCalculation.ts` to automatically deduct 30 minutes for lunch on regular working days (leaveType = #noLeave / full-day presence). The updated net hours formula must be: Net Hours = (swipeOut − swipeIn, after clamping) − 30 min lunch + 30 min breakfast bonus (if breakfastAtOffice is true). Half-day leave entries (first half and second half) must NOT have the lunch deduction applied, as per the existing rules. Full-day leave entries continue to return 0 daily hours with no deduction.

quotes: if someone is working from 7:00 to 16:00 then total working hours are 8:30 mins and lunch is 30 mins
Net Hours = (swipeOut − swipeIn) − 30 min lunch + 30 min breakfast bonus (if applicable)

acceptanceCriteria: For a No Leave day, swipe-in 07:00 and swipe-out 16:00 (no breakfast) yields exactly 8h 30m net hours.
For a No Leave day, swipe-in 07:00 and swipe-out 16:00 with breakfast yields exactly 9h 00m net hours.
Half-Day First Half entries do not have 30 minutes deducted for lunch.
Half-Day Second Half entries do not have 30 minutes deducted for lunch.
Full-Day Leave entries still return 0 daily hours.
Smart Swipe-Out Prediction and Dashboard/Analytics screens reflect the updated net hours (lunch deduction applied) correctly.
All existing unit-level logic for clamping, breakfast bonus, and leave reductions remains intact.

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Do not change the backend schema or Motoko actor.
Lunch deduction must only apply to leaveType = #noLeave (regular working day); never to half-day or full-day leave entries.
nonGoals: Changing any UI layout or visual design.
Adding configurable lunch duration (always fixed at 30 minutes).
Modifying backend data models or API.
Any new screens or features beyond the lunch deduction fix.
